skill.name: youtube-channel-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured YouTube channel search results through the BrowserAct API. The skill runs a Python helper that submits keywords and an upload-date filter, polls the BrowserAct workflow, and prints channel details returned by the service.
skill.summary: Find YouTube channels by keyword using the BrowserAct workflow API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, environment-variable access, and instructions to run a Python script. These behaviors match the stated BrowserAct API purpose, but they require user awareness because search terms and a bearer API key are sent to a third-party service. No evidence found for prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, weak cryptography, or hidden command execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Request Uses Bearer Credential
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token to the BrowserAct workflow API. This is required for the stated function, but users must trust BrowserAct and avoid exposing the key in shared terminals or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key is explicitly loaded from BROWSERACT_API_KEY and placed in an Authorization header. The destination is a declared BrowserAct API endpoint, so the concern is exposure and third-party trust rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: User Search Terms Are Sent to BrowserAct
medium_findings.description: The script accepts user keywords and an upload-date filter, then submits them as workflow input parameters. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid entering confidential campaign names, unreleased products, or private research terms.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The payload construction shows user-supplied keyword and date values going to the remote workflow. This is semantically part of the product, but it has privacy implications.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Example Requires Local Script Execution
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the agent to run a Python helper script with user-selected arguments. The command is a documented local invocation and not hidden shell execution, but marketplace users should know the skill requires script execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command appears only as a usage example for the included script. No shell metacharacter construction or dynamic command generation was found in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography and Reconnaissance Signals Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reviewed files contain no cryptographic operation and no system reconnaissance logic. The scanner likely matched ordinary words, metadata, sys stream setup, or documentation text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, host inventory, or environment enumeration beyond reading BROWSERACT_API_KEY. The referenced static signals do not match the semantic behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network and Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines local script execution, network requests, and environment credential access. The combination is legitimate for this BrowserAct API wrapper, but it should remain visible as a marketplace risk pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly show all three behaviors. Manual review did not find evidence that the combination is concealed or malicious.

content.value_statement: Manual YouTube channel research is slow and hard to structure. This skill uses BrowserAct to return channel names, links, subscriber counts, descriptions, and related metadata from keyword searches.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python helper for BrowserAct YouTube channel workflow searches.
Accepts search keywords and an optional upload-date filter.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Prints structured channel information returned by the API.
Detects missing or invalid BrowserAct API key conditions.
Supports one automatic retry for non-authorization failures.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Depends on BrowserAct API availability and workflow behavior.
May take several minutes while the remote workflow runs.
Does not verify or enrich returned YouTube data beyond the API response.

use_cases.title: Build Creator Prospect Lists
use_cases.description: Find active YouTube channels in a niche and collect channel links, descriptions, and subscriber counts for outreach planning.

use_cases.title: Map Competitor Channels
use_cases.description: Search competitor topics and summarize channels that appear in YouTube results for landscape analysis.

use_cases.title: Monitor Topic Activity
use_cases.description: Use upload-date filters to find channels recently publishing around a keyword or campaign theme.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Channel Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube channels about electric bikes. Use Any time for the upload date and summarize the returned channel data.

prompt_templates.title: Recent Creator Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube channels for home automation with the upload-date filter set to This week. Highlight active channels and subscriber counts.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Research List
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare creators in a market segment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect YouTube channels related to budget smartphone reviews. Organize the results by channel name, handle, subscribers, and description.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Prospect Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for YouTube channels about sustainable travel that uploaded recently. Identify promising outreach prospects and note any missing metadata.

output_examples.input: Search keyword: AI automation; upload date: Any time.
output_examples.output: A structured list of matching YouTube channels with channel title, URL, handle, verification status, subscriber count, description, and thumbnail link.

output_examples.input: Search keyword: fitness meal prep; upload date: This week.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of recently active channels, including subscriber scale and descriptions suitable for creator research.

output_examples.input: Search keyword: enterprise CRM tutorials; upload date: Any time.
output_examples.output: A research-ready channel table that can be reviewed for competitor tracking or partnership sourcing.

best_practices: Use specific keywords that match the audience, niche, or competitor category.
Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment before running the skill.
Review returned channel descriptions before using results for outreach decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste confidential strategy terms if they should not be sent to BrowserAct.
Do not retry repeatedly when authorization fails; check the API key first.
Do not treat subscriber counts as verified analytics without independent review.

faq.question: What does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches YouTube channels through a BrowserAct workflow using keywords and an optional upload-date filter.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The script requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to authenticate with the BrowserAct API.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return channel title, link, verification status, handle, subscriber count, description, and thumbnail URL.

faq.question: Does it run in the browser locally?
faq.answer: No. It calls a BrowserAct workflow API and polls the remote task until results are available.

faq.question: Can it handle failed tasks?
faq.answer: The skill guidance allows one retry for non-authorization failures and stops on invalid authorization.

faq.question: Is private input sent to a third party?
faq.answer: Yes. Search keywords and filters are sent to BrowserAct, so avoid confidential search terms.
